what: Broke ground on a 1,214,969 SF Class A spec building built for autonomous-vehicle logistics use.
angle: Expands Hillwood's Alliance pipeline to 8.2 MSF; sets the shallow/deep-bay rent comp for North Fort Worth.

what: Lee & Associates DFW negotiated a 35,000 SF industrial lease.

what: Closed $1.45B in financing ($800M revolver + $650M construction upsize), funding a 4th building.

what: Groundbreaking on a rail spur serving a 489-acre, up to 7 MSF park; rail service targeted Q2 2027.
angle: Outer-ring rail-served land bank; a longer-dated watch item for users priced out of core submarkets.

what: Broke ground on a two-building, 250,000 SF shallow-bay project on a 15.3-acre site near Spur 399.

what: PUCT approved group review of 75 MW+ interconnection requests; queue is ~438,000 MW, ~89% data centers.
angle: Directly paces DFW power-intensive land moving from entitlement to energized; track classification dates.

what: Directive requires data centers to fund their own grid infrastructure; PUC/ERCOT proposal due July 17, 2026.

what: Renewed and expanded to ~1 MSF through 2036, with ~$300M in planned TI and equipment spend.
